# void — ARCHITECTURE.md
# Last Updated: 2026-06-02
# Status: CURRENT SOURCE OF TRUTH — generated by ARCHITECT 2026-06-02

## Feature Overview

The Void Realm is a planned 18+ anonymous-but-DB-tracked content realm within VCSM,
designed to isolate adult content from the public realm at the realm-ID layer rather
than at the auth layer. Currently the feature consists entirely of placeholder scaffolding:
empty index files across all layers and a single stub screen displaying a "coming soon"
placeholder message. No business logic, DAL queries, controllers, or hooks exist yet.
The feature is registered in the protected route tree at `/void` and lazy-loaded from the
app router.

**Source Path:** apps/VCSM/src/features/void/
**Engine Path:** None — feature-only (no engine integration implemented)

---

| Components | NO | apps/VCSM/src/features/void/ui/index.js (empty stub) |
| Adapters | NO | apps/VCSM/src/features/void/adapters/index.js (empty stub) |
| Engine controllers | NO | None |
| Engine DALs | NO | None |

> Every subdirectory (dal/, hooks/, usecases/, adapters/, model/, screens/, ui/, api/, lib/)
> exists as a zero-byte index.js placeholder. void.js at the feature root is also a
> commented stub (export {}).

## Engine Dependencies

None — the void feature has no imports from engines/ at this time.

Platform memory records that when the Void Realm is implemented it will need to integrate
with the identity engine's is_void actor flag (currently surfaced via identity.model.js
mapProfileActor / mapVportActor helpers and the chat/setup.js actor realm resolver) and
must use resolvePublicRealmIdDAL() for all VPORT system posts.

---

## Cross-Feature Dependencies

| Feature | What Is Imported | Direction |
|---|---|---|
| — | None | — |

No cross-feature imports exist in the void feature source. The void feature is referenced
by the app router (apps/VCSM/src/app/routes/lazyApp.jsx and
apps/VCSM/src/app/routes/protected/app.routes.jsx) but those are router registrations,
not feature-to-feature imports.

Related cross-feature concern (not an import): settings/vports/dal/vports.read.dal.js
filters actors by is_void: false, enforcing the public/void realm boundary at the
settings DAL layer. chat/setup.js resolves is_void for the chat engine. These consume
the void concept but do not import from features/void/.

---

## Authorization Pattern

No authorization pattern is implemented — the current /void route is a protected route
(inside app.routes.jsx which requires authenticated session) but renders a static
placeholder. No ownership gates, actor validation, age-gating, or realm-ID resolution
exist at this time.

Required pattern when implemented: The Void Realm must gate access at the realm-ID layer.
System posts (VPORT system posts: fuel price, menu, etc.) must always resolve via
resolvePublicRealmIdDAL() and never inherit the viewer session realmId. The void:false
flag must be enforced by construction on all VPORT system posts. Age verification and DB
tracking must be implemented as a dedicated controller gate before any content read/write
reaches the DAL.

---

## Module Independence Classification

**INDEPENDENT**

The void feature currently has no imports to or from any other feature module. It is a
fully isolated scaffold with a single entry point (VoidScreen) consumed only by the router.

---

## Architecture State

**EVOLVING**

The scaffold structure was intentionally created ahead of implementation. The feature is
in a pre-build state: directory structure and route registration exist, but zero business
logic has been written. This is an EVOLVING state because the architecture contract for
this feature (realm-ID isolation, age-gate, DB tracking) is defined in platform memory
but not yet expressed in code.

---

## Known Structural Risks

1. Premature route registration: The /void route is live in the protected route tree and
   resolves to a placeholder screen. No guard prevents authenticated users from navigating
   to /void. When Void Realm content is introduced, a missing age/consent gate here would
   be a critical security gap.

2. Empty stub files: Ten of the eleven source files are zero bytes or contain only
   export {}. This creates an illusion of a structured feature that has no actual
   implementation. Any developer scanning the folder structure would assume layers exist.

3. is_void boundary not enforced in void feature itself: The is_void flag is currently
   enforced in settings/vports and chat/setup.js but there is no corresponding enforcement
   within features/void/. When void content reads/writes are built, the realm isolation
   rules must be enforced locally in this feature.

4. No SECURITY.md: Security posture for an 18+ anonymous-but-DB-tracked realm is fully
   unknown. VENOM has never run on this feature.

---

| Engine dependencies mapped | N/A | No engine deps exist yet | Will need identity engine integration |
| Tests/validation noted | FAIL | No test files, SPIDER-MAN not run | Zero test coverage |

---

## Recommended Handoffs

- VENOM — Run first. Security posture for 18+ anonymous-but-DB-tracked realm is UNKNOWN.
  Realm-ID isolation rules and the VPORT system post invariant must be audited before
  implementation begins.
- IRONMAN — Establish feature ownership before any build work starts.
- CARNAGE — When DB schema for the Void Realm is designed, CARNAGE must review the
  migration plan (realm_id columns, void flags, DB-tracked anonymous identity).
- SPIDER-MAN — No test coverage at all. Regression harness must be defined before
  implementation ships.

---

## Final Module Status

**INCOMPLETE**

The void feature is a pre-implementation scaffold. All non-trivial layers (controllers,
DALs, models, hooks, components) are empty stubs. Only the route registration and a
placeholder screen exist.
